name: UH-1 Huey Transport
prompt:
Vietnam War era Bell UH-1D/H Iroquois "Huey" transport helicopter. THE iconic helicopter of the Vietnam War.

Physical structure:
- Fuselage: distinctive teardrop/tadpole shape, wider at cockpit tapering to tail boom
  - Cockpit: two-seat side-by-side, large plexiglass windshield (4 panels) and chin bubbles
  - Cabin: open cargo bay behind cockpit, sliding doors on both sides (shown open)
  - Engine cowling: rounded hump behind/above cabin housing the T53 turboshaft
  - Exhaust: single port on top rear of engine cowling
- Main rotor: two-blade rotor on mast above engine, ~14.6m diameter (simplified, blades as flat elongated boxes)
- Tail boom: long cylindrical boom extending rearward from fuselage
  - Tail rotor: two-blade at end of boom on right side, ~2.6m diameter
  - Horizontal stabilizer: small fixed wing on left side of tail boom near tail rotor
  - Vertical fin: angled fin at tail boom end
- Landing skids: two tubular skids with cross-tubes connecting to fuselage (not wheels)
- Door guns: M60 machine guns mounted on pintle mounts at each open door
- Markings: US Army olive drab paint overall

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark rotor (0x333333), glass cockpit tint (light blue-green 0x88aaaa with glassMaterial), dark engine (0x2a2a2a)
Orientation: Sitting on skids, nose along +Z, rotors at rest
Scale: Real-world meters (fuselage ~12.7m long, ~4.4m tall to rotor hub)
Note: Build at roughly 1/10 scale for game (so ~1.27m long) OR full scale - follow the budget`,
    animations: `Main rotor spin: spinAnimation on main rotor pivot, Y axis, 1 second per revolution
Tail rotor spin: spinAnimation on tail rotor pivot, X axis, 0.3 second per revolution`
  },
  {
    name: 'UH-1C Gunship',
    slug: 'uh1c-gunship',
    category: 'vehicles/aircraft',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 5000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Bell UH-1C gunship variant. Armed attack helicopter - the "Hog" or "Frog".

Same basic Huey airframe as UH-1D but with weapon systems:
- Fuselage: same teardrop shape but with weapon pylons
  - Cockpit: two-seat, large plexiglass windshield
  - Cabin: smaller/closed compared to transport variant
  - Engine cowling: same T53 turboshaft hump
- Main rotor: two-blade, ~13.4m diameter
- Tail boom: same as UH-1D with tail rotor and stabilizer
- Landing skids: tubular skids with cross-tubes
- WEAPONS (key difference from transport):
  - Stub wings/pylons: short fixed wings on each side of fuselage, angled down slightly
  - Rocket pods: M200 19-tube 2.75" rocket pods on each pylon (cylindrical cluster)
  - XM21 subsystem: minigun mounted under nose/chin (multi-barrel rotating gun)
  - Minigun barrels: cluster of barrels pointing forward under nose

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark weapons (0x2a2a30), glass (0x88aaaa), rocket pod gray (0x555555)
Orientation: On skids, nose along +Z
Scale: Same as UH-1D transport`,
    animations: `Main rotor spin: Y axis, 1s revolution
Tail rotor spin: X axis, 0.3s revolution
Minigun spin: Z axis, 0.2s revolution`
  },
  {
    name: 'Sandbag Wall Section',
    slug: 'sandbag-wall',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'prop',
    budget: 1000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era sandbag wall fortification section. Standard firebase perimeter defense.

Physical structure:
- Three rows of sandbags stacked high (~0.45m total height)
- Wall section about 2 meters wide
- Each sandbag: roughly 0.4m x 0.15m x 0.2m, slightly rounded/bulging (use boxes with slight scale variations)
- Alternating brick pattern: bags in each row offset from row below
- Bottom row: 5 bags side by side
- Middle row: 4-5 bags offset
- Top row: 4 bags
- Bags should look slightly irregular - vary positions by +/-0.01 for realism
- Some bags slightly compressed/sagging

Colors: Sandy tan (0xc2a878), darker tan for shadow bags (0xa89060), dusty olive for some bags (0x8a7d5a)
Orientation: Wall runs along X axis, height along Y
Scale: Real-world meters (~2m wide, ~0.45m tall, ~0.4m deep)
